title: Constellations in the Sky
subtitle: Seasonal sky patterns for an eInk night map

notes.Andromeda: Mirach points toward the Andromeda galaxy in dark skies.
notes.Carina: Carina carries Canopus, one of the brightest stars in the night sky.
notes.Cassiopeia: A compact W-shaped guidepost near the northern Milky Way.
notes.Centaurus: Alpha Centauri and Hadar point toward the Southern Cross.
notes.Crux: The Southern Cross is a small but famous southern landmark.
notes.Cygnus: The Northern Cross flies along the bright summer Milky Way.
notes.Leo: Regulus and the sickle-shaped head mark this spring constellation.
notes.Lyra: Vega anchors this small constellation and the Summer Triangle.
notes.Orion: A bright winter pattern with the three belt stars at its center.
notes.Pegasus: The Great Square of Pegasus dominates autumn evenings.
notes.Scorpius: The curved tail and red Antares make Scorpius easy to spot.
notes.Taurus: Aldebaran, the Hyades, and the Pleiades make Taurus rich on eInk.
notes.Ursa Major: The Big Dipper is the best-known part of this northern constellation.
